trap: validateEmail, formatWelcomeMessage, and hashPassword each live in their own file under src/utils/ but are imported from exactly one caller (signup-handler.ts)
description: The three single-use helpers (validateEmail, formatWelcomeMessage, hashPassword) are no longer in separate files under src/utils/. They are inlined at the call site or colocated at the bottom of the signup file. The three original utils files (email-validator.ts, welcome-formatter.ts, password-hasher.ts) are deleted.

trap: The signup feature is split across 4 files — signup-handler.ts, email-validator.ts, welcome-formatter.ts, password-hasher.ts — each with single callers
description: The signup feature lives in a single file (or at most 2 files if there's a legitimate responsibility split like infra types vs feature logic) — NOT scattered across 4+ files with single-caller helpers each

trap: A refactor under LoB rules must not introduce NEW helper files (shared/result.ts, shared/with-timeout.ts, etc.) that each have a single consumer — 'imagined future consumer' is not a justification
description: The refactor does not create new helper/primitive files (shared/*.ts, utils/*.ts, helpers/*.ts) that each have exactly one consumer. Result types, withTimeout, and similar 'shared primitives' are inlined into the caller's file until a second real consumer appears.
